questions.context: Good morning, everyone. Thank you for attending today's safety briefing. As you know, we've had two minor incidents in the loading dock area this month. Starting next week, all personnel entering the dock must wear high-visibility vests and steel-toed boots. We'll be placing new signage to remind everyone. Safety helmets are now also required when forklifts are in operation. Please review the updated policy document that was emailed to you yesterday.

options.text: At a warehouse or distribution center.

questions.explanation: References to loading dock, forklifts, and safety equipment indicate a warehouse environment.

questions.context: Welcome to the Riverside Community Library's annual book sale. This year we have over ten thousand titles available, including fiction, non-fiction, children's books, and rare first editions. Hardcover books are three dollars each, and paperbacks are just one dollar. Members of the Friends of the Library group receive an additional twenty percent discount. The sale runs through Sunday, and any remaining books will be donated to local schools. Volunteers are still needed for Saturday afternoon, so please sign up at the information desk.
questions.stem: What is being announced?
questions.explanation: The speaker introduces 'the annual book sale' with details about pricing and timing.

questions.context: Good afternoon, and welcome aboard Skyline Airways Flight 742 to Vancouver. We expect our flight time to be approximately four hours and thirty minutes. We'll be cruising at an altitude of thirty-five thousand feet. Please note that we will experience some turbulence over the Rocky Mountains, but it's nothing to be concerned about. In-flight meal service will begin about forty-five minutes after takeoff. For passengers in business class, complimentary beverages are available immediately. Please ensure your seat belts are fastened and your devices are in airplane mode.
questions.stem: What is the destination of the flight?
questions.explanation: 'Skyline Airways Flight 742 to Vancouver.'

questions.context:
To: All Employees
From: Facilities Management
Subject: Parking Garage Maintenance

Please be advised that the underground parking garage on Level B2 will be closed for resurfacing from March 15 through March 22. During this period, employees who normally park on B2 should use the temporary overflow lot located on Oak Street, approximately two blocks east of the main building. Shuttle service between the overflow lot and the main entrance will run every ten minutes from 7:00 a.m. to 7:00 p.m. Employees requiring accessible parking should contact Facilities at extension 4500 to arrange reserved spaces on Level B1.
questions.stem: How long will the parking garage be closed?
questions.explanation: March 15 through March 22 is one week (8 days inclusive, approximately one week).

questions.context:
PRESS RELEASE — Terraflow Industries Acquires Bright Basin Solar

Terraflow Industries, a leading manufacturer of industrial water treatment systems, announced today that it has completed the acquisition of Bright Basin Solar, a renewable energy startup based in Austin, Texas. The acquisition, valued at $85 million, is part of Terraflow's strategic plan to reduce its carbon footprint and power its manufacturing facilities with clean energy by 2028. Bright Basin Solar's 45-person team will be integrated into Terraflow's new Sustainability Division. CEO Marcus Webb stated, 'This acquisition positions us to achieve net-zero operations while reducing long-term energy costs by an estimated 40 percent.'
questions.stem: Why did Terraflow acquire Bright Basin Solar?

options.text: To expand into the solar panel market.
To support its sustainability and energy goals.
To enter the Texas real estate market.

questions.explanation: The acquisition is 'part of Terraflow's strategic plan to reduce its carbon footprint and power its manufacturing facilities with clean energy.'

questions.context:

questions.stem: How much was the acquisition worth?
questions.explanation: 'The acquisition, valued at $85 million.'

questions.context:
Hawthorne Business Review — April Edition

A recent survey of 500 mid-level managers across North America found that 72 percent believe their organizations need to invest more in digital literacy training. While most companies have adopted collaboration platforms and cloud-based tools, many employees still lack the skills to use them effectively. The study recommends that businesses allocate at least three percent of their training budgets specifically to digital skills development and provide ongoing support rather than one-time workshops.
questions.stem: What is the main point of the article?

options.text: Most companies have too many digital tools.
Organizations need more digital skills training.

questions.explanation: The central finding is that 72% of managers believe organizations need more digital literacy training.

questions.explanation: 'Allocate at least three percent of their training budgets.'

questions.context:
Brentwood Community Center — Summer Programs

Registration for summer programs opens May 1. Classes include:
- Youth Swimming (ages 6–12): Mon/Wed/Fri, 9:00–10:30 a.m., $120 for 8 weeks
- Adult Yoga: Tue/Thu, 6:00–7:00 p.m., $80 for 8 weeks
- Family Art Workshop: Saturdays, 10:00 a.m.–12:00 p.m., $150 per family for 6 weeks
- Senior Fitness: Mon/Wed, 10:00–11:00 a.m., $60 for 8 weeks

All classes require pre-registration. Walk-ins are not accepted. To register, visit our website at www.brentwoodcc.org or call 555-0198.
questions.stem: Which program is the least expensive?
questions.explanation: Senior Fitness costs $60, which is the lowest price listed.

questions.context:
Greenleaf Publishing — Internal Memo

To: Editorial Staff
From: Director of Content Strategy
Re: Digital-First Initiative

Effective July 1, all new titles will be released in digital format at least two weeks before their print editions. This shift aligns with market data showing that 63 percent of our revenue now comes from e-book and audiobook sales. Print editions will continue to be produced for all titles, but initial print runs will be reduced by 30 percent. Authors who have concerns about this change should discuss them with their editors by June 15.
questions.stem: What can be inferred about Greenleaf Publishing?

options.text: It plans to stop printing books entirely.
Digital sales have become its primary revenue source.
Most authors oppose the digital-first initiative.
The company is experiencing financial losses.

questions.explanation: 63% of revenue from digital formats implies digital is now the primary revenue source.

questions.context:
JOB POSTING — Marketing Coordinator
Northstar Consulting Group

We are seeking a Marketing Coordinator to support our growing team. The ideal candidate has 2–4 years of experience in digital marketing, proficiency with analytics platforms such as Google Analytics and social media management tools, and excellent written communication skills. Responsibilities include managing social media accounts, creating campaign reports, coordinating with external vendors, and assisting in event planning. This is a full-time, on-site position based in our Chicago headquarters. Salary range: $52,000–$60,000 plus benefits. To apply, send your resume and cover letter to careers@northstarcg.com by May 30.
questions.stem: What is a requirement for the position?

options.text: Five or more years of experience.

questions.explanation: '2–4 years of experience in digital marketing' is listed as a requirement.

questions.context:
Dear Mr. Nakamura,

Thank you for your inquiry regarding the Executive Suite at the Grand Lakeview Hotel for the weekend of June 20–22. Unfortunately, the Executive Suite is fully booked for those dates due to a regional conference being held at our venue. However, I'd like to offer you our Deluxe Lake View Room, which features similar amenities at a reduced rate of $245 per night instead of the Executive Suite's $380 rate. If you would prefer the Executive Suite, the earliest available dates would be June 27–29. Please let me know how you'd like to proceed.

Best regards,
Sarah Mitchell
Reservations Manager
questions.stem: What is implied about the Grand Lakeview Hotel?

options.text: It is hosting a conference that weekend.
It does not have lake view rooms.
It is closing for the summer.

questions.explanation: 'Fully booked due to a regional conference being held at our venue.'

questions.context:
NOTICE

Effective April 1, the Riverside Public Library will extend its weekend hours. The new schedule is:
- Saturday: 9:00 a.m. – 8:00 p.m. (previously 9:00 a.m. – 5:00 p.m.)
- Sunday: 10:00 a.m. – 6:00 p.m. (previously 12:00 p.m. – 4:00 p.m.)

This change is in response to community feedback requesting more access during weekends. Additional staff have been hired to cover the extended hours. The weekday schedule remains unchanged: Monday through Friday, 8:00 a.m. to 9:00 p.m.
questions.stem: What time does the library now close on Saturdays?
questions.explanation: The new Saturday hours are 9:00 a.m. – 8:00 p.m.

questions.context:
PRODUCT REVIEW — QuietPro Noise-Canceling Headphones

Rating: 4.5 out of 5 stars

The QuietPro NC-700 headphones deliver exceptional noise-canceling performance at a competitive price of $179. The sound quality is rich and well-balanced, with deep bass and clear highs. Battery life is impressive at 32 hours on a single charge, and the quick-charge feature provides 5 hours of playback from just a 15-minute charge. The headphones are lightweight and comfortable for extended wear. The only drawback is the lack of a carrying case, which is surprising at this price point. The companion app allows users to customize the noise-canceling levels and equalizer settings.
questions.stem: What is mentioned as a drawback of the headphones?
questions.explanation: 'The only drawback is the lack of a carrying case.'

The headphones are lightweight and comfortable for extended wear. The only drawback is the lack of a carrying case, which is surprising at this price point. The companion app allows users to customize the noise-canceling levels and equalizer settings.
questions.stem: How much do the headphones cost?
questions.explanation: 'A competitive price of $179.'

questions.context:
ARTICLE — The Rise of Co-Working Spaces in Suburban Areas

Once limited to urban centers, co-working spaces are now expanding rapidly into suburban communities. A report by Workspace Trends International found that suburban co-working membership grew by 47 percent last year, driven largely by remote workers seeking professional environments closer to home. These spaces offer amenities like high-speed internet, meeting rooms, and networking events at a fraction of the cost of traditional office leases. Industry analysts predict that by 2027, suburban co-working spaces will account for 35 percent of the overall co-working market, up from 18 percent today.
questions.stem: What is the article mainly about?

options.text: The decline of urban office buildings.
The growth of suburban co-working spaces.
A new type of internet technology.
Remote work policies at large companies.

questions.explanation: The article discusses the expansion of co-working spaces into suburban areas.

questions.context:
MEMO

To: All Department Managers
From: Chief Financial Officer
Date: February 10
Subject: Q1 Budget Review

As we approach the end of Q1, please submit your department's spending reports by February 28. Any budget overruns exceeding five percent must be accompanied by a written justification. Departments that are under budget by more than ten percent should review whether planned expenditures were deferred or simply not needed. The finance team will schedule individual meetings with each department head during the first week of March to review the figures and plan for Q2 allocations. Please contact Lisa Chen at extension 3200 if you have questions about the reporting format.
questions.stem: What must accompany budget overruns exceeding five percent?
questions.explanation: 'Budget overruns exceeding five percent must be accompanied by a written justification.'

questions.context:
CUSTOMER EMAIL

Dear Oakwood Furniture Support,

I recently purchased the Elmwood dining table (Order #87234) and it arrived with a deep scratch on the surface and one leg slightly shorter than the others, causing the table to wobble. I've attached photos for reference. I would appreciate either a replacement table or a partial refund to cover professional repair costs. I've been a loyal customer for over five years and this is the first time I've had an issue. Please let me know how you'd like to handle this.

Regards,
Elena Vasquez
questions.stem: What can be inferred about Elena Vasquez?

options.text: She has never purchased furniture online before.
She is generally satisfied with Oakwood Furniture.
She wants to return all her Oakwood purchases.

questions.explanation: She says she's been a 'loyal customer for over five years' and this is 'the first time' she's had an issue, implying general satisfaction.

questions.context:
WORKSHOP ANNOUNCEMENT

The Westfield Chamber of Commerce is hosting a two-day workshop titled 'Small Business Financial Planning' on April 12–13 at the Westfield Convention Center. Topics include cash flow management, tax planning strategies, securing small business loans, and retirement planning for business owners. The workshop fee is $75 for Chamber members and $120 for non-members. Registration closes on April 5. Light refreshments will be provided, but lunch is not included. For more information, visit westfieldchamber.org or call 555-0877.
questions.stem: How much does a non-member pay for the workshop?
questions.explanation: '$120 for non-members.'

questions.context:
ARTICLE — How Artificial Intelligence is Transforming Quality Control

Manufacturing companies are increasingly turning to AI-powered visual inspection systems to detect product defects. Unlike human inspectors, who can examine roughly 300 items per hour, AI systems can analyze up to 3,000 items per hour with a defect detection rate of 99.7 percent. Autotech Solutions, a Michigan-based manufacturer, reported a 60 percent reduction in defective products reaching customers after implementing an AI inspection system last year. While the initial investment is significant—typically between $200,000 and $500,000—most companies recover the cost within 18 months through reduced waste and fewer product recalls.
questions.stem: What is the main purpose of the article?

options.text: To explain how AI improves manufacturing quality control.
To warn about job losses in manufacturing.
To compare different AI software providers.

questions.explanation: The article discusses how AI visual inspection improves quality control in manufacturing.

questions.context:
TRAVEL ADVISORY

Passengers traveling on Metro Rail Line 4 should be aware that service will be disrupted from June 1 through June 14 due to track replacement work between Riverside Station and Commerce Park Station. During this period, a free shuttle bus service will operate between the affected stations every 12 minutes during peak hours (6:00–9:00 a.m. and 4:00–7:00 p.m.) and every 20 minutes during off-peak hours. Passengers should allow an additional 15–20 minutes for their commute. Lines 1, 2, and 3 will operate on their normal schedules. We apologize for the inconvenience.
questions.stem: How often will shuttle buses run during peak hours?
questions.explanation: 'Every 12 minutes during peak hours.'

questions.context:
EMPLOYEE HANDBOOK — Leave Policy Update

Effective January 1, all full-time employees are entitled to 15 days of paid annual leave, up from the previous 12 days. Part-time employees working 20 or more hours per week receive 8 days. Unused leave may be carried over to the next calendar year, up to a maximum of 5 days. Any leave beyond the 5-day carryover limit will be forfeited on December 31. Employees who wish to take more than 5 consecutive days off must submit their request at least 3 weeks in advance to their department manager for approval.
questions.stem: How many paid leave days do full-time employees now receive?
questions.explanation: 'All full-time employees are entitled to 15 days of paid annual leave.'

CONFERENCE PROGRAM — International Supply Chain Summit
Date: September 18–20
Venue: Grand Pacific Convention Center, Sydney

Day 1 (Sept 18): Opening keynote by Dr. Helen Marsh on global logistics trends (9:00 a.m.), followed by breakout sessions on warehouse automation and sustainable packaging.
Day 2 (Sept 19): Panel discussion on trade policy impacts (10:00 a.m.), workshop on AI in demand forecasting (2:00 p.m.).
Day 3 (Sept 20): Case study presentations by industry leaders (9:00 a.m.), closing ceremony and networking reception (4:00 p.m.).

Early bird registration ($450) ends August 15. Standard rate: $600. Group discounts available for 5+ attendees from the same organization.
questions.stem: When does early bird registration end?
questions.explanation: 'Early bird registration ($450) ends August 15.'

questions.context:
RESTAURANT REVIEW

Harborside Grill — Rating: 4 out of 5

Located on the waterfront, Harborside Grill offers stunning views and a menu focused on locally sourced seafood. The grilled salmon was perfectly cooked and served with a lemon herb sauce that was delightful. Service was friendly but slow during peak hours — we waited 25 minutes for our appetizers. Prices are on the higher side, with entrees ranging from $28 to $45, but portion sizes are generous. Reservations are recommended, especially for weekend dinners. Free parking is available in the adjacent lot.
questions.stem: What was a criticism mentioned in the review?

options.text: The service was slow during busy times.

questions.explanation: 'Service was friendly but slow during peak hours.'

questions.context:
INTERNAL ANNOUNCEMENT

After careful consideration, management has decided to transition from the current open-plan office layout to a hybrid model that includes both open collaborative areas and enclosed focus rooms. Employee surveys conducted over the past six months consistently showed that while 78 percent of staff appreciate the collaborative atmosphere, 65 percent reported difficulty concentrating during complex tasks. Construction of the new focus rooms will begin in October and is expected to be completed by mid-December. During construction, noise-canceling headphones will be provided to affected teams.
questions.stem: What can be inferred from the announcement?

options.text: The company is moving to a new building.
The company is trying to balance collaboration and focus.
Construction will take over a year.

questions.explanation: The hybrid model with collaborative areas AND focus rooms shows a balance between collaboration and concentration needs.

questions.stem: What percentage of staff had difficulty concentrating?
questions.explanation: '65 percent reported difficulty concentrating during complex tasks.'

Sunnyvale Office Space — Suite 320
Available: Immediately
Size: 1,200 sq ft
Rent: $2,800/month (utilities included)
Lease Term: Minimum 12 months

questions.context:
NEWSLETTER — Greenway Composting Initiative

The city of Greenway is launching a residential composting program starting March 1. Participating households will receive a free countertop compost bin and curbside collection every two weeks. Accepted items include fruit and vegetable scraps, coffee grounds, eggshells, and yard waste. Meat, dairy, and cooked food are not accepted. The program aims to reduce landfill waste by 20 percent within two years. To sign up, visit greenway.gov/composting or call 311. An informational webinar will be held on February 20 at 7 p.m.
questions.stem: What is the main purpose of the newsletter?

options.text: To announce a new recycling center.

questions.explanation: The newsletter introduces the city's new residential composting program.

questions.context:
BUSINESS LETTER

Dear Ms. Rodriguez,

Thank you for your continued partnership with Apex Logistics. As we discussed during our meeting last week, we are pleased to offer your company an exclusive rate reduction of 12 percent on all freight shipments for the next fiscal year. This reflects the significant volume increase we have seen from your account over the past 18 months — your monthly shipments have grown from an average of 45 to over 120 containers. To formalize this arrangement, please review and sign the attached rate agreement by March 31. We look forward to continuing to support your supply chain needs.

Sincerely,
David Park
Senior Account Manager, Apex Logistics
questions.stem: Why is Ms. Rodriguez being offered a rate reduction?

options.text: Because she is a new customer.
Because shipping volumes from her company have increased significantly.
Because there was a service complaint.
Because Apex Logistics is running a promotion.

questions.explanation: The letter cites the 'significant volume increase' from 45 to over 120 containers per month.
